locations.description_short: Interior suite with drawing room and bedroom where Hadji Murad dictates his life story.

locations.description_short: Leather-scented quarters where Gamzalo, Khan Mahoma, Eldar and Khanefi lodge.
locations.dramatic_use: Reveals diverging loyalties among Hadji Murad's followers

locations.description_short: Small mountain aoul, 'size of an ass's head', Hadji Murad's birthplace.
locations.dramatic_use: Origin of Hadji Murad and site of Akhmet Khan's later siege

locations.description_short: Seat of the Avar Khans where Hadji Murad grew up in palace intimacy.
locations.dramatic_use: Stage of the Khans' massacre and Hamzad's assassination

locations.description_short: Royal residence of the Avar Khans inside Khunzakh, site of courtly childhood.
locations.dramatic_use: Setting for dynastic intrigue and Hamzad's takeover

locations.description_short: Simple mountain dwelling where Hadji Murad's mother nursed and hid her scar.
locations.dramatic_use: Sensory flashback of maternal sacrifice and lullaby

locations.description_short: Remote farm where the silver-hammering grandfather lived and received the child.
locations.dramatic_use: Memory of ancestral craft and kinship

locations.description_short: Water source beneath Tselmess where the boy clung to his mother's trousers.
locations.dramatic_use: Tender childhood image anchoring mother motif

locations.description_short: Murid encampment on a hill where Hamzad received and murdered the Khans.
locations.dramatic_use: Site of treacherous ambush that reshapes Hadji Murad's fate

locations.description_short: Village mosque where Hadji Murad and Osman assassinated Hamzad during feast.
locations.dramatic_use: Blood-revenge climax against Hamzad's murids

locations.description_short: Cliffside path with hundred-and-twenty-yard abyss where Hadji Murad leaped.
locations.dramatic_use: Iconic escape that scars his leg and makes his legend

locations.description_short: Russian military garrison town; destination of Hadji Murad's chained transport.
locations.dramatic_use: Intended Russian prison aborted by his leap

locations.description_short: Mountainous homeland of the Avars contested between Khans, Shamil and Russians.
locations.dramatic_use: Political stake behind Hadji Murad's shifting allegiances

locations.description_short: Wider Caucasian region where many Naibs oppose Shamil covertly.
locations.dramatic_use: Political field Vorontsov hopes to turn via Hadji Murad

locations.description_short: Imperial frontier theater of perpetual war debated in Tiflis and Petersburg.
locations.dramatic_use: Strategic abstraction weighed by Vorontsov, Chernyshov and Nicholas

locations.description_short: Forested enemy territory targeted by Nicholas's strategy of forest-felling raids.
locations.dramatic_use: Geographic object of imperial cordon policy

locations.description_short: Russian fortress near Chechnya chosen as base for Hadji Murad's family negotiations.
locations.dramatic_use: Operational launchpad for ransom scheme

locations.description_short: Fort near hostile settlement ruled out because Vorontsov's son no longer commands it.
locations.dramatic_use: Rejected alternative underscoring kunak bond with young Vorontsov

locations.description_short: Preferred frontier line where Hadji Murad offers to serve the Russians.
locations.dramatic_use: Proposed theater of his promised service

locations.description_short: Administrative district commanded by Prince Tarkhanov, consulted on Hadji Murad.
locations.dramatic_use: Bureaucratic reference situating Tarkhanov

locations.description_short: Region Shamil dispatched Hadji Murad to, where he seized sheep and horses.
locations.dramatic_use: Pretext for Shamil's suspicion and final rupture

locations.description_short: Khanate ruled in turn by Mahomet-Murza then Akhmet Khan under Russian appointment.
locations.dramatic_use: Source of Akhmet Khan's hatred driving the betrayal

locations.description_short: Imamate-held highlands where Hadji Murad's mother, wives and son are captive.
locations.dramatic_use: Invisible prison motivating every negotiation

locations.description_short: Imperial capital where Chernyshov delivers the Vorontsov report to Nicholas.
locations.dramatic_use: Seat of autocratic power deciding Hadji Murad's fate

locations.description_short: Petersburg residence of Nicholas I with rebuilt rooms and ministerial cabinet.
locations.dramatic_use: Architectural symbol of Nicholas's vanity and power

locations.description_short: Lofty four-windowed audience room with Alexander I portrait and writing table.
locations.dramatic_use: Chamber of capricious imperial decrees on Hadji Murad and the Pole

locations.description_short: Antechamber where Chernyshov and Dolgoruky are met by the aide-de-camp.
locations.dramatic_use: Threshold ritual emphasizing courtly servility

locations.description_short: Hall where hundred courtiers await Nicholas's New Year greetings.
locations.dramatic_use: Spectacle of autocratic self-display

locations.description_short: Court chapel where the deacon chants 'Many Years' for Nicholas.
locations.dramatic_use: Religious mirror of imperial self-worship

locations.description_short: Gallery Nicholas traverses en route to Minister Volkonski's apartments.
locations.dramatic_use: Transit beat weaving through palace routine

locations.description_short: Formal dining room where Nicholas discusses Hadji Murad with ambassadors.
locations.dramatic_use: Site of the 'two cauteries' political bon mot

locations.description_short: Public masked ball with reserved velvet box for the Emperor's trysts.
locations.dramatic_use: Scene of Nicholas's predatory encounter with the Swedish girl

locations.description_short: Spartan chamber with hard bed and Napoleon-comparison cloak.
locations.dramatic_use: Private space of self-mythologizing insomnia

locations.description_short: Petersburg quayside walk in 13-below frost where Nicholas meets the student.
locations.dramatic_use: Public stage of Nicholas's petty cruelty and narcissism

locations.description_short: Side entrance of the Winter Palace where Helena Pavlovna's carriage arrives.
locations.dramatic_use: Trigger for Nicholas's contempt of liberal relatives

locations.description_short: Petersburg ballet where Nicholas orders a diamond ring for a dancer.
locations.dramatic_use: Closing beat of imperial appetite and whim

aliases: place where Nicholas usually had rendezvous
locations.description_short: Designated secret lodging where Nicholas takes women for assignations.
locations.dramatic_use: Hidden venue of the Emperor's hypocrisy

locations.description_short: Western border where Nicholas orders two divisions staged against unrest.
locations.dramatic_use: Geographic proof of Nicholas's reactionary politics

locations.description_short: Long sled relay where couriers beat drivers bloody to deliver reports.
locations.dramatic_use: Physical toll connecting Caucasus and throne

notes: Large stretches of chapters 11 and 13 are Hadji Murad's oral flashbacks dictated in the Tiflis lodging; locations like Khunzakh, Tselmess, Hamzad's Camp, Mansokha Path are accessed via flashback mode, not present-time travel. Chapter 14 is almost entirely a written dispatch (epistolary), so locations mentioned (Grozny, Vozdvizhensk, Lesghian Line, Shoushin District, Daghestan) appear as referenced rather than enacted spaces. Chapter 15 shifts sharply to Petersburg and introduces a dense sub-system of Winter Palace interiors. The Murids' Room is structurally a sub-room of Hadji Murad's Lodging and could be compressed into a single 'Tiflis lodging' composite for screen. Masquerade Hall and Rendezvous Apartment are spatially distinct from the Palace. Shamil's Mountains is an abstract off-screen region carrying the emotional weight of the captive family.
